question_text: Planet A has a gravitational field strength of 8 N/kg and Planet B has a gravitational field strength of 2 N/kg. A 60 kg astronaut travels from Planet A to Planet B. Explain why the astronaut's mass stays the same but their weight changes, and calculate both weights.
correct_answer: Mass remains 60 kg (unchanged). Weight on Planet A: 480 N. Weight on Planet B: 120 N. Mass is the amount of matter in an object and doesn't change with location. Weight is the gravitational force on that mass, which depends on local gravitational field strength.
explanation: An astronaut's mass (60 kg) is an intrinsic property that doesn't change when moving between planets. However, weight depends on the local gravitational field strength. On Planet A: W = 60 × 8 = 480 N. On Planet B: W = 60 × 2 = 120 N. The astronaut feels much heavier on Planet A because its stronger gravity pulls harder.
